claim: MCP servers can use HTTP transport (not just STDIO) to expose tools via web endpoints, enabling easier deployment and avoiding config file complexity for users.
actionable: Deploy MCP servers as HTTP endpoints (e.g., /mcp URL) rather than STDIO executables to reduce friction for non-technical users adding servers to agents.
source_title: Introducing WebMCP: Agents in the Browser

claim: MCP Apps are single-file HTML bundles with embedded CSS/JS/fonts that agents can render in iframes, enabling rich interactive UIs beyond chat.
actionable: When building agent interfaces, create MCP apps for complex interactions (navigation, image galleries) rather than forcing everything through text responses.
source_title: Introducing WebMCP: Agents in the Browser

claim: MCP apps are sandboxed in iframes with no local storage, no network access, and restricted async calls; this prevents state leakage but requires careful design.
actionable: When building MCP apps, embed all resources (fonts, images, CSS) as base64 or data URLs; use the call_server tool for any external requests.
source_title: Introducing WebMCP: Agents in the Browser

claim: MCP Resources allow agents to pre-load large documents (full transcripts, documentation) into context, but many agents don't implement resource support yet.
actionable: Design MCP resources for documentation/corpus access (not tool calls), but provide fallback tool-based access until agent harnesses widely support resources.
source_title: Introducing WebMCP: Agents in the Browser

claim: Chat-only ('starfish') interfaces burden users with discovery; rich UI with visual cues and interactive elements is better for non-expert users and agentic experiences.
actionable: Design agent interfaces with visual hierarchy, guidance, and interactive surfaces rather than defaulting to chat; chat is to agentic UX what CLI was to desktop UX.
source_title: Introducing WebMCP: Agents in the Browser

claim: WebMCP (proposed standard) lets browser agents call functions directly on websites by reading tool attributes from HTML form elements, avoiding screenshot parsing and DOM traversal.
actionable: Add 'tool-name' and 'tool-description' attributes to HTML forms; define imperative tools via navigator.modelContext.registerTools for direct function calls from agents.
source_title: Introducing WebMCP: Agents in the Browser
